beneficiaries.rationale: Lockheed Martin is the prime contractor for F-35, PAC-3/THAAD missile defense, and LRASM — all directly relevant to a Pacific/Taiwan contingency. Heightened China-war rhetoric accelerates DoD Pacific Deterrence Initiative funding and FMS demand from Japan/Australia/Taiwan.

RTX makes Tomahawk, SM-6, AIM-120, and Patriot — munitions explicitly being restocked under Pacific contingency planning. Raytheon has backlog visibility tied directly to munitions replenishment narratives that intensify on China war headlines.
L3Harris has direct exposure to Pacific ISR, tactical comms, and the Tomahawk propulsion (via Aerojet). Smaller-cap defense prime with higher beta to geopolitical escalation headlines than LMT/RTX.

Huntington Ingalls is the sole builder of US nuclear aircraft carriers and a key Virginia-class submarine builder — both central to any Pacific naval buildup narrative. Pure-play naval shipbuilder benefits most from Pacific war discussion.

Hormuz disruption + Pacific escalation fears drive oil risk premium; Exxon has the largest upstream leverage among US majors and benefits from any Strait-of-Hormuz / South China Sea shipping risk repricing.
Palantir's Maven/TITAN contracts and Gotham are framed as AI-warfare infrastructure; retail and momentum flows rotate into PLTR on escalation headlines as a high-beta defense-tech proxy.
Kratos builds Valkyrie unmanned combat aircraft and target drones explicitly pitched for Pacific loyal-wingman missions; small-cap with highest headline beta to Pacific war narratives.

macro_events.event_summary: Singapore's foreign minister publicly warned that the Strait of Hormuz crisis is a "dry run" for a potential US-China war in the Pacific, elevating Taiwan/South China Sea tail risk in mainstream discourse.

losers.rationale: Las Vegas Sands derives the vast majority of EBITDA from Macau and Singapore — both directly in the blast radius of a US-China Pacific conflict scenario. First to sell on Taiwan/China war escalation headlines.
Wynn Resorts has ~70% of property EBITDA from Macau; same Taiwan-Strait risk exposure as LVS and trades as a direct pair on China conflict fears.
Taiwan Semi is the single most-exposed US-listed ADR to a Pacific war — fabs physically in Taiwan. Any headline elevating Taiwan invasion probability repricing drives TSM lower on risk premium.

Nike has ~15% revenue from Greater China plus significant Vietnam/Asia manufacturing; Pacific conflict scenario disrupts both demand and supply chains. Consumer discretionary most exposed to a China decoupling narrative.

Apple's supply chain is ~80% concentrated in China/Taiwan assembly and has ~19% China revenue exposure — the textbook single-stock loser in a Pacific war tail-risk repricing despite its megacap status.
Shanghai Gigafactory produces ~50% of Tesla's global deliveries and China is ~22% of revenue; any US-China decoupling escalation directly threatens unit economics.

ZIM Integrated Shipping's network is heavily Asia-US Pacific trans-Pacific lanes; a Pacific war scenario shuts down its highest-margin routes. Pure-play container shipper with direct Pacific exposure.
Yum China (KFC/Pizza Hut China) is a pure-play China consumer name listed in the US — directly hit on any Taiwan/Pacific war headline as an ADR risk-off proxy.

primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium on Taiwan Strait / South China Sea conflict
Defense budget reallocation toward Pacific Deterrence munitions and naval platforms
China-revenue and Taiwan-supply-chain exposure repricing in multinationals
headlines: Hormuz is just a ‘dry run’ if China and U.S. go to war in the Pacific, Singapore foreign minister warns
